content.claim: Cross-national causal evidence on school closure and population decline is contested: Polish village-level studies found smaller structural social-capital decline where schools were saved, while Finnish and Swedish studies found school closure correlated with or unrelated to population decline respectively.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Conflicting findings across national contexts suggest institutional/demographic heterogeneity not captured by single causal mechanism
Polish studies measure social capital as outcome, not population retention directly
Reverse causality and selection bias (schools close where depopulation is already occurring) not adequately disentangled in cited work
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
